logic_bridge: Discourse is the social frequency; Conduct is the interpersonal direction.

Extroverted.discourse: Buoyant, animated, and verbally expansive; maintains high engagement and thrives on back-and-forth dialogue.
Extroverted.conduct: Initiates interaction and keeps the flow moving; draws energy from the group and actively seeks to include others.

Introverted.discourse: Measured, contemplative, and gentle; utilizes deliberate pauses and prefers depth over verbal volume.
Introverted.conduct: Draws energy from quiet and one-on-one connection; listens more than they speak and prioritizes meaningful presence over chatter.

Friendly.discourse: Warm, welcoming, and approachable; uses inclusive, easygoing language to dissolve social tension.
Friendly.conduct: Creates an immediate sense of emotional safety; treats strangers and friends alike with inviting, non-judgmental energy.

Selective.discourse: Discerning, thoughtful, and reserved; speaks with a careful "wait-and-see" quality until trust is established.
Selective.conduct: Maintains social boundaries; observes carefully and only opens up once a genuine, tested connection is formed.

Takes Initiative.discourse: Proactive, clear, and directive; speaks with an "action-first" vocabulary that suggests solutions and paths forward.
Takes Initiative.conduct: Naturally leads the social direction; steps up to provide clarity during confusion and suggests activities without hesitation.

Supportive.discourse: Other-focused, encouraging, and uplifting; uses language designed to build the other person up rather than centering themselves.
Supportive.conduct: Acts as the primary anchor for others; focuses energy on the needs of the room and proactively offers help or validation.

Independent.discourse: Self-sufficient and autonomous; speaks with a self-directed quality that doesn't seek external validation or agreement.
Independent.conduct: Follows their own internal compass; supports others but maintains emotional autonomy and doesn't rely on social approval.

Surly.discourse: Unfriendly, terse, and unenthusiastic; uses minimal words, flat tones, and "curt" responses to signal disinterest.
Surly.conduct: Maintains a difficult social barrier; avoids deep engagement and reacts to enthusiasm with unimpressed or annoyed detachment.
